key_insight: A non-overlapping Schwarz FE-neural operator coupling with Neumann-Dirichlet exchange makes hybrid solid mechanics simulation more efficient and geometry-flexible, while keeping physics consistency through analytically derived strain/stress operators.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate-accelerated finite element analysis for localized nonlinearities, irregular domains, and long-time dynamics in solid mechanics.
method: Replace overlapping DD exchange with non-overlapping ND Schwarz coupling
Use Point-DeepONet on unstructured FE point clouds for irregular geometries
Derive strain and stress from displacement operators analytically to reduce parameters
evidence_signal: The framework is validated on three benchmarks—static linear elasticity, quasi-static hyperelasticity, and elastodynamics—across regular and irregular geometries, with reduced inner Schwarz iterations and bounded error growth over time.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable interface coupling and accurate traction transfer, so implementation must carefully manage Schwarz convergence and boundary flux consistency on complex meshes.
paper_title: A Non-Overlapping Schwarz Hybrid Finite Element-Neural Operator Framework for Solid Mechanics on Irregular Domains
